@@ -0,0 +1,97 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: de80
+project: spatial-nav
+title: 'Enter-to-activate: bind Enter on board cards (inspect) and perspective tabs (switch)'
+---
+## What
+
+Establish "Enter = activate the focused scope" consistently across all focusable scopes. Each scope type has its own idiomatic activation:
+
+| Scope type | Enter action | Status |
+|---|---|---|
+| LeftNav button | `view.switch:<id>` | ✅ done (`left-nav.tsx:168-178`, `view.activate.<id>`) |
+| Perspective tab | switch perspective (`setActivePerspectiveId(p.id)`) | ❌ **this task** |
+| Grid body cell | `grid.edit` / `grid.editEnter` (enter edit mode) | ✅ done (`grid-view.tsx:271-272`) |
+| Inspector field row | `inspector.edit` / `inspector.editEnter` | ✅ done (`inspector-focus-bridge.tsx:39-48`) |
+| Row selector | `ui.inspect` with target | ✅ done (`data-table.tsx:1120-1132`) |
+| Board / entity card | `ui.inspect` with target | ❌ **this task** |
+
+This task closes the two remaining gaps.
+
+### Gap 1 — Perspective tabs
+
+`kanban-app/ui/src/components/perspective-tab-bar.tsx:290-310` — `ScopedPerspectiveTab` wraps its `PerspectiveTab` in a `FocusScope` with `commands={[]}`. There is no Enter binding, so pressing Enter on a focused tab falls through to the parent scope chain and does nothing useful.
+
+The existing click path is `onSelect={() => setActivePerspectiveId(p.id)}` (perspective-tab-bar.tsx:211), which is already correct. Enter needs to run the same handler.
+
+**Pattern to mirror**: LeftNav's `ViewButton` (`left-nav.tsx:150-180`) uses a per-instance namespaced command `view.activate.<id>` with `execute: handleClick`. Do the same for perspective tabs — `perspective.activate.<id>` bound to Enter with `execute` calling the existing `onSelect`.
+
+```tsx
+// ScopedPerspectiveTab — build per-tab commands array
+const commands = useMemo<CommandDef[]>(() => [{
+  id: `perspective.activate.${perspective.id}`,
+  name: `Activate ${perspective.name}`,
+  keys: { vim: "Enter", cua: "Enter", emacs: "Enter" },
+  execute: onSelect,
+  contextMenu: false,
+}], [perspective.id, perspective.name, onSelect]);
+```
+
+Wire `commands={commands}` on the `FocusScope`.
+
+### Gap 2 — Board / entity cards
+
+`kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-card.tsx` — cards render an `InspectButton` (line 294) that dispatches `ui.inspect` with an explicit `target: moniker` on click. Keyboard Enter has no binding; the card's `FocusScope` receives `commands={useEntityCommands(...)}`, but `ui.inspect` in `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/ui.yaml` has **no `keys:` entry** — so the schema-driven command loader in `entity-commands.ts:110-142` produces a `ui.inspect` entry with no key binding.
+
+Two possible fix sites. Pick option A:
+
+- **Option A (preferred)** — add `keys: { vim: Enter, cua: Enter, emacs: Enter }` to `ui.inspect` in `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/ui.yaml`. Every scope that includes `ui.inspect` in its commands now gets Enter-to-inspect automatically. The existing shadow mechanism (child-scope Enter bindings like `inspector.editEnter`, `grid.editEnter`, `view.activate.<id>`, `perspective.activate.<id>`, row selector's explicit `ui.inspect`) protects the edge cases — inner Enter bindings win over the global `ui.inspect` Enter.
+  - Cross-check each scope that has `ui.inspect` in scope. Scopes with their own Enter command: LeftNav buttons (view.activate.X), perspective tabs (perspective.activate.X, after Gap 1), grid cells (grid.edit/editEnter at grid level), inspector rows (inspector.edit at inspector level), row selectors (ui.inspect at leaf with explicit target). Cards have no competing Enter binding → newly get Enter-to-inspect. ✅
+  - Risk: any scope where `ui.inspect` becomes in-scope but Enter should do something else — audit via `useEntityCommands` consumers.
+
+- **Option B (fallback)** — if Option A breaks something, narrow to a per-card local command on the `EntityCard` `FocusScope` (add to the `useEntityCommands` `extraCommands` argument with an explicit Enter binding). Logic duplicates with InspectButton's click handler but scope is minimal.
+
+**Principles at play**: per `CLAUDE.md` memory, the codebase's `command-organization` rule says cross-cutting commands live once in YAML and are not re-listed per-entity. `ui.inspect` is already the cross-cutting inspect command; adding its Enter binding at the YAML level is idiomatic.
+
+### Files to modify
+
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/perspective-tab-bar.tsx` — add `commands` array to `ScopedPerspectiveTab`'s FocusScope (Gap 1)
+- `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/ui.yaml` — add `keys: { vim: Enter, cua: Enter, emacs: Enter }` to `ui.inspect` (Gap 2, Option A)
+- If Option B is chosen: `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-card.tsx` instead — add `extraCommands` for Enter binding
+
+### Out of scope
+
+- LeftNav Enter (already done, `01KPQXEMJEGVY7JF9HM5JSWTAP` is redundant and can be closed)
+- Row selector Enter (already done, `01KPRGXFE2EE2SQZQB2PR63N2X` is redundant and can be closed)
+- Column header Enter → sort (separate concern, file later if desired)
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] Focus a perspective tab via spatial nav, press Enter → the active perspective switches to that tab
+- [ ] Focus a board / entity card via spatial nav, press Enter → inspector opens for that card's entity
+- [ ] Focus a grid cell, press Enter → edit mode activates (existing — regression-protected)
+- [ ] Focus an inspector field row, press Enter → field enters edit mode (existing — regression-protected)
+- [ ] Focus a row selector, press Enter → inspector opens for that row's entity (existing — regression-protected)
+- [ ] Focus a LeftNav button, press Enter → view switches (existing — regression-protected)
+- [ ] No accidental double-fires (e.g. pressing Enter on a card doesn't both inspect AND trigger an unrelated parent-scope action)
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/perspective-tab-bar.test.tsx` (create if missing, or extend existing) — render the tab bar with 2 perspectives, focus the second tab, dispatch Enter, assert `setActivePerspectiveId` was called with the second perspective's id
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-card.test.tsx` (or the equivalent board fixture test) — render a card, focus it, dispatch Enter, assert `ui.inspect` was dispatched with `target` equal to the card's entity moniker
+- [ ] Regression: existing `kanban-app/ui/src/components/data-table.test.tsx` tests for Enter-on-cell edit and row-selector Enter-inspect must still pass
+- [ ] Regression: existing `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspector-focus-bridge.test.tsx` (if present) tests for Enter-on-field-row edit must still pass
+- [ ] Run `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test` — all 1301+ tests pass, 2 new tests green
+- [ ] Run `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-commands` — YAML change doesn't break backend command registry tests
+- [ ] Manual: in each scope (LeftNav button, perspective tab, board card, grid cell, inspector field, row selector), focus via spatial nav and press Enter → verify the correct per-scope action fires
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — write the two failing Enter-activation tests first (perspective tab, board card), then implement.
+- Start with Gap 1 (perspective tabs) — it's a straightforward local binding and gives you a working pattern to reference.
+- For Gap 2, prefer Option A (YAML key). If tests surface an unexpected scope conflict, fall back to Option B and document why in the task's progress notes.
+- **Close two redundant tasks after this lands** (or during, if convenient): `01KPQXEMJEGVY7JF9HM5JSWTAP` (LeftNav Enter — already implemented in code) and `01KPRGXFE2EE2SQZQB2PR63N2X` (row selector Enter — already implemented in code).
+
